etymology.description: 行(行) (xíng, "street; path") as semantic component + 朮(朮) (zhú, "glutinous millet") as phonetic -> denotes paths or methods
etymology.interpretation: The character 術(術) combines the meaning of a path or course, represented by 行(行) (xíng, "street; path"), with the phonetic cue from 朮(朮) (zhú, "glutinous millet") to convey the concept of methods or techniques like following a path.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form 术(術) retains the core meaning without including the full representation of 行(行), streamlining the strokes while partially maintaining the phonetic element of 朮(朮).

examples.chinese: 他的艺术水平很高(他的藝術水平很高)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yìshù shuǐpíng hěn gāo.
examples.english: His artistic skill is very high.

examples.chinese: 医生采用了新术(醫生採用了新術)，病人很快康复(病人很快康復)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng cǎiyòngle xīn shù, bìngrén hěn kuài kāngfù.
examples.english: The doctor used a new technique, and the patient recovered quickly.

examples.chinese: 学习一种新技能(學習一種新技能)需要时间(需要時間)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéxí yī zhǒng xīn jìnéng xūyào shíjiān.
examples.english: Learning a new skill takes time.
